what_it_does: This file is a coverage ledger that maps the 35 Feature-Atlas capability classes and their 123 affordances to resource entries and per-op statuses, reporting which capabilities are reached by a real endpoint (building), which are contracted but backend-less (planned), and which are unmapped or out-of-scope.
observations: Machine-headlined counts report 13 covered classes, 22 planned-only classes, and 0 unmapped classes; 45 of 123 affordances are building and 78 are planned-only.
Layer 1 (contracted claims) is populated with per-class tables mapping affordances to ops, entries, and statuses; Layer 2 (demonstrated proof) is explicitly empty because no driving harness has run.
The file includes a post-R1.4 correction that reverses previously claimed building statuses after a command-wrapper layer was removed as wrong-approach.
It separates backend-gaps (buildable company endpoints that do not yet exist) from out-of-local-scope boundaries (host, org, or Anthropic surfaces the fabric deliberately does not drive).
A silent-gap watch section flags places coverage could be overstated and marks each as clear, flagged, or named rather than silent.
It claims perfect bidirectional closure: all 123 defined affordances are reached by at least one op, and no op claims a phantom affordance.
No code symbols are present; the file is prose, tables, and frontmatter only.
standouts: The file self-corrects stale coverage claims after the wrapper removal, keeping the ledger honest.
It explicitly keeps the demonstrated layer at zero and refuses to mark anything live.
It names every gap and boundary rather than leaving classes silently unmapped or falsely covered.
It treats coverage as a planning instrument (gap pressure) rather than a completion metric.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter typed as contract-coverage with build, captured date, status, source-of-truth, and honesty rules.
Separates a machine-owned COVERAGE:MACHINE-BEGIN/END block from hand-derived prose.
Represents status values as building, planned, or live, with building affordances bolded in tables.
Maps each class to affordances, ops, resource entries, and statuses in Markdown tables.
Cites other contract files such as FEATURE-ATLAS.md, OUT-OF-SCOPE.md, INVENTORY-EXCLUSIONS.md, and resources/*.md.
Follows CONTRACT-FORMAT honesty rules: building requires a real cited endpoint, planned means contracted or out-of-scope.
concerns: Some prose is admitted as stale regarding CC-04/CC-29/CC-32/CC-34 being reopened as building config-face ops while still listed as out-of-scope exclusions elsewhere.
The file is hand-derived because tools/coverage.py is not yet built, so counts depend on manual grep verification and may drift.
Layer 2 is empty, meaning all contracted coverage claims remain unproven by task verdicts or load data.
The repeated corrections and reversals suggest earlier versions contained inconsistent claims, requiring trust in the latest regeneration note.
notes: A manually maintained, machine-headlined coverage map that reports 13 covered and 22 planned-only Feature-Atlas classes, names every backend gap and scope boundary, and keeps the demonstrated layer empty.
questions: What exactly was the Session Fabric R1.4 wrapper layer that was removed, and which specific ops did it affect?
Where is tools/coverage.py and when will it be implemented to regenerate the machine block automatically?
What is the drive-real-session path that is expected to flip planned-only classes back to covered?
How is the building status verified beyond parsing frontmatter status and atlas tags?
What is the precise boundary between a company backend gap and a native Anthropic surface that the fabric should not bridge?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to serve as the F9.3 gap-pressure instrument mapping Feature-Atlas classes to resource entries with honest status and named gaps; the file fulfills that purpose while also self-correcting prior overclaims and separating scope boundaries, though it remains hand-derived until the coverage generator exists.
apparent_themes: Gap pressure as a build-planning instrument
Clear separation between company fabric scope and host/org/Anthropic scope
No silent holes and no green-painted coverage
Machine-owned headline counts separated from human-authored interpretation
intention_signals: The file is trying to enforce an honest, auditable contract between the capability fabric's implemented backend surface and the full feature atlas, making build priorities and scope boundaries explicit and preventing premature claims that a capability is done.
novelty: It combines a machine-headlined coverage matrix with explicit planned-only and out-of-local-scope categories, and includes a self-correcting reversal of coverage flips after a wrapper layer was deleted — treating coverage as an honest ledger rather than a completion dashboard.
contribution: It contributes a structured, status-labeled coverage map with named gaps and scope boundaries, plus a template for separating contracted claims from demonstrated proof, which a fused system could reuse for build planning and scope communication.
summary_for_embedding: Coverage ledger mapping 35 Feature-Atlas classes and 123 affordances to resource entries with building or planned status, naming backend gaps and out-of-scope boundaries and leaving the demonstrated layer empty.
intention_for_embedding: To provide an honest, auditable gap-pressure instrument for the capability fabric build by exposing which capabilities have real endpoints, which are contracted but backend-less, and which lie outside local scope.
